channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Career Justice Department officials reassigned to different positions: reports
item.description: The U.S. Justice Department reportedly moved several senior officials to other divisions ahead of the Senate's anticipated confirmation of President Trump's attorney general pick.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Coast Guard surging assets to Gulf of America, other waterways to support Trump’s executive orders
item.description: In support of President Trump's executive orders, the Coast Guard will send additional resources to maritime borders, the military branch said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pete Hegseth’s former sister-in-law alleges abuse against second wife in affidavit to Senate
item.description: Senators vetting the nomination of Minnesotan Pete Hegseth for defense secretary received an affidavit Tuesday from a former sister-in-law alleging that the onetime Fox News host was abusive to his second wife, to the point where she feared for her safety. Hegseth denies the allegations.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Estimated 24.6 million TV viewers watched inauguration coverage, smallest audience since 2013
item.description: The Nielsen Company says an estimated 24.6 million people watched coverage of President Donald Trump's second inauguration.
item.dc:creator: David Bauder, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump administration throws out policies limiting migrant arrests at sensitive spots like churches
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Officers enforcing immigration laws will now be able to arrest migrants at sensitive locations like schools and churches after the Trump administration threw out policies limiting where those arrests could happen as the new president seeks to make good on campaign promises to carry
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pete Hegseth's former sister-in-law alleges abuse against second wife in affidavit to Senate
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Senators vetting the nomination of Pete Hegseth for defense secretary received an affidavit Tuesday from a former sister-in-law alleging that the onetime Fox News host was abusive to his second wife, to the point where she feared for her safety. Hegseth denies the allegations.
item.dc:creator: FARNOUSH AMIRI and TARA COPP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Henderson mayor threatens legal action against councilwoman following ‘spurious rumors’ of extramarital affair
item.description: A litany of personal attacks has enveloped the city of Henderson, and elected officials are coming forward to explain why distrust has taken over a part of city hall.
item.dc:creator: James Schaeffer, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Donald Trump open to Elon Musk buying TikTok
item.description: TikTok restored service to U.S. users Sunday afternoon and credited Trump for pushing to keep the app operational.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Key career officials at Justice Department reassigned to different positions, AP sources say
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Justice Department has reassigned key senior officials across multiple divisions as part of a leadership shakeup ahead of the expected confirmation of President Donald Trump's pick for attorney general, Pam Bondi, multiple people familiar with the matter said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Republican senators are mostly quiet on Trump's sweeping pardons of Jan. 6 rioters
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Many Republican senators say they do not agree with President Donald Trump’s decision to pardon and commute sentences for more than 1,500 people who assaulted police officers, broke into the building or committed other crimes related to the Jan.
item.dc:creator: MARY CLARE JALONICK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: U.S. throws out policies limiting arrests of migrants at sensitive locations like schools, churches
item.description: Officers enforcing immigration laws will now be able to arrest migrants at sensitive locations like schools and churches after the Trump administration threw out policies limiting where those arrests could happen.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump administration directs all federal diversity, equity and inclusion staff be put on leave
item.description: President Donald Trump’s administration is moving to end affirmative action in federal contracting and is directing that all federal diversity, equity and inclusion staff be put on paid leave and eventually be laid off.
item.dc:creator: Alexandra Olson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Mayor Adams says he won't publicly criticize President Trump
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- Mayor Eric Adams said Tuesday he would not criticize President Donald Trump publicly. Adams also doggedly defended his decision to attend Trump’s inauguration Monday and skip local Martin Luther King Jr.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Democratic AGs sue Trump over birthright citizenship executive order
item.description: Twenty-two Democrat-led states and two cities challenged President Trump’s executive order restricting birthright citizenship, that on Tuesday kicked off the first legal battles between his new Justice Department and Democratic attorneys general.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court won’t take up fight over Pennsylvania mail ballot date requirement
item.description: The Supreme Court said Tuesday it won’t hear civil rights groups’ appeal seeking to invalidate Pennsylvania's dating requirement for mail ballots, which has invalidated thousands of votes each recent election and become a central legal fight over voting in the key swing state.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Trump pumps brakes on Day 1 tariffs
item.description: Trump campaigned hard on tariffs, which allowed him to tap into undercurrents of frustration about the globalized economy.
item.dc:creator: Tobias Burns, NewsNation

item.title: Trump pardons upend massive Jan. 6 prosecution by freeing rioters and dismissing cases
item.description: Rioters locked up for their roles in the Jan. 6 Capitol attack have been released, while judges have begun dismissing dozens of pending cases after President Donald Trump’s sweeping grant of clemency to all 1,500-plus people charged in the insurrection.
item.dc:creator: Alanna Durkin Richer And Michael Kunzelman, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Elon Musk already has a White House email address
item.description: "It seems to confirm he's going to have an EEOB office," the source said, using the acronym for Eisenhower Executive Office Building.
item.dc:creator: Amie Parnes, NewsNation

item.title: Expert: Trump can’t override Minnesota state law with executive orders
item.description: University of Minnesota political science professor Kathryn Pearson says President Donald Trump can’t direct states in matters that are up to state law.
item.dc:creator: Nina Moini and Ellie Roth, MPR News

item.title: What is an executive order? A look at Trump's tool for quickly reshaping government
item.description: An incoming president signing a flurry of executive orders is standard practice. Executive orders allow a president to wield power without action from Congress. But there are also limits to what orders can achieve.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Just minutes before leaving the presidency, Biden pardons his siblings and their spouses
item.description: The pardons, announced just minutes before Biden left office, capped a slew of unprecedented presidential action by the Democrat, who has been known as an intuitionalist during his half-century in politics.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump to make historic move towards revoking birthright citizenship
item.description: Trump wants to reinterpret the phrasing of the 14th Amendment to mean that the federal government would not recognize automatic birthright citizenship for children born in the U.S. to parents without legal status.
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo, MPR News

item.title: Indigenous activist Leonard Peltier granted clemency by President Biden
item.description: The outgoing Biden administration has released Leonard Peltier from prison. Numerous activists and tribal officials have requested the release of Peltier, whom they believe to be innocent of killing two FBI agents in 1975.
item.dc:creator: Allison Herrera and Melissa Olson, MPR News

item.title: Trump to issue 10 executive orders cracking down on border
item.description: President-elect Trump is set to sign off on 10 executive orders Monday, moving swiftly to crack down on immigration and drug cartels during his first day in office with a blitzkrieg of immigration actions.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Trump to promise 'new era of national success' in inaugural address
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump is set to promise a “new era of national success” in his inaugural address, according to The Wall Street Journal.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, Fox 5

item.title: Biden pardons Fauci, Jan. 6 committee ahead of Trump inauguration
item.description: (The Hill) -- President Joe Biden granted pardons to several prominent public servants Monday who have faced attacks from President-elect Donald Trump in one of his final acts in office. Biden issued pardons for Dr.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: Giuliani reaches tentative settlement with Georgia election workers
item.description: Attorneys for Rudy Giuliani and two former Georgia election workers said Thursday they have reached a tentative settlement, a remarkable twist that would end the workers’ extensive litigation to collect their $146 million defamation judgment.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: Cortez Masto leads Nevada Democrats in renewed effort against nuclear waste dump
item.description: Nevada's Democratic U.S. senators and representatives in Congress reintroduced the Nuclear Waste Informed Consent Act on Wednesday, gearing up for possible renewed efforts to put a nuclear dump in the state.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: DeSantis outlines immigration plan for lawmakers to consider ahead of special session
item.description: During a stop at the Polk County Sheriff’s Office, Gov. Ron DeSantis proposed a list of statewide immigration measures ahead of a special session.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Tampa Bay native Pam Bondi goes through Senate confirmation hearings to become U.S. Attorney General
item.description: Pam Bondi grew up in Temple Terrace, she worked in the Hillsborough County State Attorney's office as a prosecutor, and she served two terms as the elected Florida State Attorney General.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Patterson, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Rubio vows to place US interests 'above all else' as Trump's top diplomat
item.description: Florida Sen. Marco Rubio promised on Wednesday to implement President-elect Donald Trump’s “America First” vision as secretary of state, vowing in his confirmation hearing that the incoming administration will forge a new path by placing American interests “above all else.
item.dc:creator: FARNOUSH AMIRI and MATTHEW LEE Associated Press, WFLA, NBC
